references.title: Predictive Modelling of Student Reviewing Behaviors in an Introductory Programming Course
references.journal: Proceedings of International Conference of Educational Data Mining Computer Science Education

extracted.Data sources:
LMS logs,
Exam Performance,
Quiz Grades
extracted.Features: [Number of Assessments, Average Time to Review, Distinct Days, Distinct Actions, Number of Interactions, Quiz 6 Mark, Quiz 10 Mark]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Target is computed as average of the grades obtained on each Exam.
extracted.Comments: Authors create 3 different models - 1 per exam. Results show that the more data is used - later moment of prediction the better the performance.
extracted.Limitations:
Final grade is computed as average of all exam scores. 

Requires students to fail first.

extracted.Data sources:
LMS logs,
Exam Performance,
Quiz Grades
extracted.Features: [Number of Assessments, Average Time to Review, Distinct Days, Distinct Actions, Number of Interactions, Quiz 6 Mark, Quiz 10 Mark]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Target variable is grade based - above 77.60% (high) vs below (low).

Authors create a classifier to predict the exam performance on each of the 3 exams.
extracted.Models:
Logistic Regression, 
kNN,
Decision Tree, 
SVM, 
SVM (Gaussian kernel), 
Random Forest
extracted.Comments: Authors identify that different features are relevant depending on the period. Review Patterns and Time to Attend Review.

annotations.text: Authors created predictive models that were trained on student reviewing behavior with the goal of identifying students in need of support. This is a retrospective analysis that shows reasonable accuracy, suggesting potential to develop actions that help students form effective reviewing strategies.
